prompt:
Rotation on the relay hosts is currently 'someone sshs in and deletes things', which is how we lost the June logs. I want a planner we can run under -WhatIf during change freezes and for real otherwise. Two hard rules from the incident review: the script NEVER reads the wall clock — the reference instant comes in as an argument — and every file it touches goes through proper ShouldProcess gating, so -WhatIf is trustworthy by construction, not by code review.

Build `rotaplan.ps1` in the repo root:

    pwsh -NoProfile -File rotaplan.ps1 -Path <dir> -Now <iso8601-utc> [-KeepCount <n>] [-MaxAgeDays <n>] [-WhatIf]

Candidates are the `*.log` and `*.log.gz` files directly in -Path (no recursion); everything else is invisible to the tool. Age is -Now minus the file's LastWriteTimeUtc; 'older than N days' means STRICTLY more than N*24 hours — a file exactly N days old is not older. Policy, in this order:

- any candidate older than -MaxAgeDays (default 30): delete.
- of the surviving `.log` files, the newest -KeepCount (default 3) stay uncompressed: keep. Newest by mtime; on equal mtimes the ordinal-earlier name counts as newer. Every other surviving `.log`: compress.
- surviving `.log.gz` files: keep.

The plan prints on stdout in apply mode AND under -WhatIf: one `<action> <name>` line per candidate, sorted ordinal by name. Apply mode then executes it: delete removes the file; compress writes `<name>.gz` containing the gzip (System.IO.Compression.GZipStream) of the exact original bytes and removes the original; keep touches nothing. Declare SupportsShouldProcess and gate each mutation on $PSCmdlet.ShouldProcess — under -WhatIf the harness ignores pwsh's own 'What if:' chatter but the plan lines must be exact and the directory byte-identical afterwards.

Errors (stdout stays empty): a -Now that won't parse -> stderr `rotaplan: bad -Now value: <value>`, exit 64; missing -Path -> `rotaplan: path not found: <path>`, exit 66. A directory with no candidates prints nothing, exit 0.

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_rotaplan.ps1` from the workspace root, unmodified. Don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P4 item 5: log-rotation planner. Flagship PowerShell-native pin: [CmdletBinding(SupportsShouldProcess)] + $PSCmdlet.ShouldProcess (source-grepped like ps-haulstats greps Group-Object) with -WhatIf semantics verified behaviorally — probed 2026-07-16: 'What if: Performing the operation ...' lines land on STDOUT of a child pwsh, so the harness strips 'What if: '-prefixed lines before the byte-exact plan compare, requires the What-if chatter to mention both a compress and a delete target (proves the gate is consulted per mutation), and diffs a full name|mtimeTicks|base64 tree snapshot to prove nothing moved. Determinism: -Now is an ARGUMENT (wave law: never Get-Date), all mtimes set via SetLastWriteTimeUtc; strict '>' age boundary pinned by exactly.log at exactly 30.0 days; equal-mtime tie pinned by a.log/b.log. Apply mode round-trips gzip via .NET GZipStream (probe-verified). Differentiation: go-logrotate is a Go library around size-based rotation; bash-scratchprune prunes by fixed rules with bash; neither has a dry-run gating idiom — ShouldProcess/-WhatIf has no bash/py analog and is the catalog's stated point.
